story.community_prompt: Which nature topic should Wild Brief explain next after plant?
story.cta_prompt: Want to see how trees quietly engineer the planet?
story.description: A clip of plants, leaves, flowers, or plant movement: a plant with roots in the ground
story.hook: Plant shows the movement before the payoff
story.lead: Roots feel the ground shake before you see it move. Watch the soil shift right where the roots are. That's because plants sense vibrations through their roots. Tiny hairs on the tips pick up the tiniest tremors. So next time you see a plant, remember it’s already listening. What if trees could warn each other about storms?

story.description: A clip of marine life in the ocean: vibrant underwater life in aquarium exhibit
story.hook: Sharks show the gills before the payoff.
story.lead: Sharks can pause right there. Watch this one's gill slits flare—not because it’s moving, but because it’s breathing. Most sharks ram water over their gills because they can’t pump it themselves. But this one. It’s using its cheeks like bellows. That’s why it can just hang there, perfectly still. Neat trick, huh.
